comment.body: Alma, I’ve been working my way through Natenberg the last few weeks—highlighter and pencil in hand (it’s a study, not a read)—and give a sincere “thank you” for the recommendation.  It is so well written, and the clarity and careful progression through the subject matter naturally build from one topic to the next.  I will add that one problem for the non-professional is the problem of too much choice and not being equipped to discern the right choice from the wrong choice, except for learning the hard way.  So while there is a wealth of free information and books are truly cheap for their value, there’s also a wealth of mediocre and just plain bad information also (especially on the internet, and especially on investment/trading), and this raises the problem of what books are worth reading, what internet sources are worthy of consideration.  That’s where your recommendations—even for free material or $50 books—are good as gold and equally as valuable.  I reiterate:  a sincere thank you for your book recommendations and of course for your work on the substack.

comment.body:
I ask myself, how much of Alma's short term analysis is still valid, as it was built around a soft NFP print, and it ended up being hot. 

Dovish expectations for June sank rapidly, as market knows Powell is more concerned about inflation and will only cut when he sees clear signs. 

Next week FOMC will most certainly not included any rate cuts, but it will be important what is said about the recent numbers and tariffs and how the Fed reads them.

comment.body: That’s what I don’t get about the whole thing with tariffs and coming down. Your entire selling point is replacing tax revenue with tariff revenue. On that basis alone you can’t remove tariffs without either humongous concessions across the board with biggest trading partners or they stick as a baseline and other negotiations take place for non tariff factors. I struggle to understand the outcome even with the geo aspect of things. What becomes the new normal if his best case scenario happened to play out? Weaker China with better domestic production etc

comment.body: I think tariffs to level the playing field makes sense.  In that, eventually you get to every country lowering trade barriers.  The biggest problem IMO, is that this could be a long-term negotiation, and China probably figures it just has to outlast Trump's 3 more years.  Makes the next presidential election really interesting.
